title: viola bridgesii
intro: Viola bridgesii, also known as the mountain pansy or Bridges' violet, is a species of flowering plant in the viola genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and improving respiratory issues, and has been used to treat conditions like bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Viola bridgesii include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or extracting the active compounds using a solvent like ethanol.
